name: next-intl (Internationalization for Next.js)

performance.notes: Zero runtime overhead for static messages; RSC loads only needed locale

agent_readiness.notes: i18n library built for Next.js App Router (also supports Pages Router). useTranslations('namespace') in client, getTranslations() in RSC. ICU message syntax for plurals, dates, numbers. Locale-aware routing via middleware and [locale] dynamic segment. TypeScript integration extracts message keys from JSON files. next-intl/navigation for locale-aware Link/redirect. Supports deeply nested namespaces. The standard choice for Next.js i18n over react-i18next for App Router.

gotchas: App Router and Pages Router have different APIs
TypeScript message key extraction needs build step
Large message files increase bundle if not split by namespace
best_when: App Router projects with RSC and client components mixed
Teams wanting TypeScript type safety on translation keys
avoid_when: Simple apps with only 1-2 languages (manual strings may be simpler)
Projects already using react-i18next (not worth migrating)
summary: next-intl is the standard i18n library for Next.js App Router. useTranslations() in client, getTranslations() in RSC. ICU message syntax for plurals/dates/numbers. Locale routing via middleware and [locale] segments. TypeScript key inference. Deeply nested namespaces. The go-to choice for Next.js i18n over react-i18next when using App Router.
